title: 10 best herbal tinctures for jaw clenching

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kava tinctures helps with jaw clenching because it targets the underlying anxiety and stress that often contribute to teeth grinding. The plant's active compounds, including kavalactones, work to calm the nervous system, reducing feelings of tension and promoting relaxation. As a result, individuals who use kava tincture may experience a decrease in jaw clenching and teeth grinding, leading to improved overall oral health and reduced discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects: Kava's ability to calm anxiety helps alleviate muscle tension, including jaw clenching, by reducing feelings of stress and unease.
Muscle relaxant properties: Kava contains compounds that can relax muscles, including those in the face and jaw, which can help reduce or eliminate jaw clenching caused by tension.

Analgesic (pain-relieving) effects: Kava's pain-relieving properties can help alleviate headaches, facial pain, and discomfort associated with chronic jaw clenching, making it a natural treatment for temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the kava plant contain a high concentration of kavalactones, which are responsible for its anxiolytic and relaxant properties, making them effective in reducing jaw clenching.

Rhizomes: Rhizomes, the underground stems of the kava plant, have been used traditionally to treat muscle tension and pain, including jaw clenching, due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh piper methysticum roots in the morning after dew has dried.
Chop the harvested roots into small pieces and dry them for at least 24 hours.
Combine one part of the dried root material with two parts of high-proof vodka and store mixture for four weeks shaking daily.
After four weeks filter the mixture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove solids and discard any remaining plant material.
Transfer the filtered tincture into dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and label as 1:2 kava root in ethanol.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop tinctures helps with jaw clenching because of its unique blend of herbs that work together to ease tension in the jaw muscles. The antispasmodic properties of passionflower, a key ingredient in maypop tincture, help to relax the muscles and reduce spasms, while the adaptogenic properties of ashwagandha help to calm the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that often contribute to jaw clenching. The result is a natural relief from tension headaches and facial pain caused by clenched jaws.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic Properties: Maypop tincture's antispasmodic properties help to relax and calm tense muscles, including those in the jaw, reducing spasms and pain associated with jaw clenching.
Analgesic Properties: The analgesic compounds found in maypop tinctures help to numb or reduce pain in the jaw and surrounding areas, providing relief from discomfort caused by jaw clenching.

Anxiolytic (Anti-Anxiety) Properties: Maypop tincture's anxiolytic properties can help to reduce stress and anxiety, common contributors to jaw clenching, promoting a sense of relaxation and reducing the frequency and intensity of jaw tension.
remedy_parts: Roots: Maypop roots contain a unique combination of bioactive compounds, such as alkaloids and glycosides, which help to relax tense muscles and reduce jaw pain.

Barks: The barks of the maypop plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and discomfort associated with jaw clenching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality maypop passionflower vines passiflora incarnata for maximum potency and freshness.
Dry the harvested vines in a warm dark place for seven to ten days to preserve the botanicals.
Combine one part dried maypop passionflower vine with two parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar filling it almost to the top.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for four to six weeks allowing the botanicals to extract into the vodka.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean dark glass bottle discarding the solids and label the tincture with date dosage and preparation instructions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tinctures helps with jaw clenching because its calming properties can effectively soothe muscle tension in the face, jaw, and neck. The herb's sedative effects can help to relax the temporomandibular joint (TMJ), reducing the frequency and severity of clenching and grinding habits. As a natural stress reliever, valerian tinctures can also alleviate anxiety and promote relaxation, further contributing to a decrease in jaw tension and discomfort. By addressing underlying causes of bruxism, valerian tinctures offer a holistic approach to managing jaw clenching and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Sedative and anxiolytic effects: Valerian's calming properties help reduce anxiety and stress, which can contribute to jaw clenching by relaxing the muscles and reducing tension.
Muscle relaxant: Valerian has a natural ability to relax muscle contractions, including those in the jaw, which can help alleviate jaw clenching and related symptoms like TMJ pain and stiffness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Valerian's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce swelling and inflammation in the jaw joint and surrounding tissues, which can exacerbate jaw clenching and other oral health issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in essential oils, roots of valerian plant are used to create a sedative and relaxing tincture that can help alleviate symptoms of jaw clenching.
Rhizomes: Valerian rhizomes contain sesquiterpenes, which contribute to the plant's calming properties, making them ideal for creating a tincture that soothes jaw tension.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh valeriana officinalis roots and dry them thoroughly for at least two weeks in a warm dark place.
Grind dried valerian roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine one part of the powdered valerian root with one part of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar and store it in a cool dark place for four weeks.
Shake the jar twice daily for four weeks to facilitate extraction of active compounds from the valerian roots into the ethanol.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle and label it with the date and ingredients used for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tinctures helps with jaw clenching because of its calming and relaxing properties. The herb's essential oils, particularly linalool and linalyl acetate, work to reduce muscle tension and stress in the face and neck, which can contribute to jaw clenching and bruxism. As a natural anxiolytic, lavender tincture helps to soothe anxiety and promote relaxation, allowing individuals to release physical tension and relax their jaw muscles, thereby reducing the frequency and severity of jaw clenching episodes.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties: English lavender tincture helps to calm anxiety and stress, which is often a major contributor to jaw clenching.
Muscle relaxant properties: Lavender's calming effects on the nervous system also help to relax tense muscles in the face and jaw, reducing symptoms of bruxism (jaw clenching).

Inflammation-reducing properties: English lavender tincture contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and pain associated with jaw tension and clenching.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in calcium and magnesium, which can help relax muscles and alleviate tension in the jaw.
Seeds: Containing a high amount of mucilage, a soothing substance that can calm inflammation and discomfort in the jaw.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and leaves in sufficient quantity for the desired dosage.
Combine one part dried herbs by weight with two parts vodka at a ratio of 1:2 and store in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for six weeks, shaking the jar daily, to extract active compounds.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids after pressing out excess tincture.
Store the resulting english lavender tincture in an amber glass bottle with a dropper lid and use within six months for optimal potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Brahmi tinctures helps with jaw clenching because of its ability to calm the mind and nervous system. The herb's adaptogenic properties help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for jaw clenching. By promoting relaxation and reducing mental tension, Brahmi tinctures can help alleviate physical symptoms such as teeth grinding and jaw pain. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties may also help soothe irritated muscles and joints in the face and neck, further reducing discomfort and stiffness associated with chronic jaw clenching.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic effects: Brahmi tincture's adaptogenic properties help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers of jaw clenching and tension headaches.

Neuroprotective and antispasmodic properties: Brahmi's neuroprotective compounds, such as brahmine and amalaki, have been shown to relax muscle spasms and reduce pain in the jaw, reducing symptoms of jaw clenching and temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders.

Anxiolytic and sedative properties: The anxiolytic and sedative compounds present in Brahmi tincture, such as flavonoids and saponins, can help calm the nervous system, reducing muscle tension and promoting relaxation of the jaw muscles, which can alleviate symptoms of jaw clenching and teeth grinding.
remedy_parts: Roots: Known for their anti-inflammatory properties, roots are used to reduce swelling and pain in the jaw caused by clenching.
Barks: The barks contain flavonoids that help to relax muscles and alleviate tension in the jaw and facial areas.
Seeds: Rich in antioxidants, seeds are used to soothe and calm irritated nerves and tissues associated with jaw clenching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality bacopa monnieri leaves and stems in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the plant material in a warm, dark place for at least 7-10 days or using a food dehydrator at 70°f.
Crush the dried plant material into small pieces using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder to release oils and resins.
Soak the crushed plant material in high-proof grain alcohol such as vodka or everclear at a ratio of 1 part herb to 2 parts solvent for 4-6 weeks.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and storing the tincture in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ashwagandha tinctures helps with jaw clenching because it possesses adaptogenic properties that help to reduce stress and anxiety, two primary triggers of jaw tension. The herb's ability to calm the nervous system can relax the facial muscles, releasing tension in the jaw and alleviating symptoms of bruxism, also known as teeth grinding or clenching. Additionally, ashwagandha's anti-inflammatory properties may help to soothe any resulting pain and discomfort, promoting a sense of relaxation and reducing the frequency and severity of jaw clenching episodes.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Ashwagandha's adaptogenic properties help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for jaw clenching.
Muscle relaxant properties: The herb contains compounds that have a natural muscle-relaxing effect, which can help to ease tension in the jaw muscles and alleviate jaw clenching.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Ashwagandha's anti-inflammatory effects can help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the jaw joint and surrounding tissues, which may contribute to jaw pain and clenching.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in adaptogenic compounds, ashwagandha roots help reduce anxiety and stress-related jaw clenching.
Barks: The barks contain withanolides, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can ease tension in the jaw muscles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality ashwagandha roots and wash them thoroughly under running water.
Dry the ashwagandha roots in a low-temperature oven or food dehydrator at 150°f for 24 hours.
Chop the dried ashwagandha roots into small pieces and macerate them in high-proof vodka at a ratio of 1 part herbs to 5 parts solvent for 2 weeks.
Strain the tincture mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids.
Store the ashwagandha tincture in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, keeping it away from direct sunlight and heat.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with jaw clenching because of its calming and soothing properties, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety that often contribute to teeth grinding and clenching. The herbal remedy contains apigenin, an antioxidant that binds to GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension. Additionally, chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties can help alleviate any discomfort or pain associated with jaw clenching, allowing for a more relaxed and comfortable oral structure.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (anti-anxiety) properties: Chamomile helps to calm the mind and body, reducing tension and stress that can contribute to jaw clenching.
Sedative effects: The sedative properties of chamomile can help to relax the muscles in the face and jaw, reducing spasms and clenching associated with teeth grinding or bruxism.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory effects may help reduce pain and inflammation caused by chronic jaw clenching, which can lead to conditions like temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the chamomile plant are used due to their rich content of apigenin, a flavonoid that helps to relax muscles and reduce anxiety, making it effective in soothing jaw clenching.
Flowers: The flowers of chamomile contain bisabolol, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate tension and discomfort in the jaw area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers in full bloom for optimal potency.
Cleanly dry the harvested flowers using a food dehydrator at 40°c for 24 hours.
Crush the dried chamomile flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder.
Combine one part of the powdered chamomile with two parts of high-proof vodka in a glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep in a dark place at room temperature for 6-8 weeks, shaking daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with jaw clenching because it contains menthol, a natural analgesic that relaxes tense muscles. When taken internally as a tincture, peppermint oil can ease jaw tension by reducing inflammation and soothing irritated tissues. Additionally, the cooling sensation of peppermint can help distract from pain and discomfort, providing quick relief from jaw clenching caused by stress, anxiety, or dental issues. As a result, herbal peppermint tinctures offer a natural and effective way to calm jaw tension and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Digestive-soothing compounds: Peppermint's menthol and menthone content helps to relax digestive muscles, which can also calm tense jaw muscles and reduce clenching.
remedy_parts: Roots: Peppermint roots contain compounds that help relax muscles and reduce tension, making them effective in alleviating jaw clenching.
Barks: The barks of peppermint plants have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe irritated jaw joints and relieve pain associated with jaw clenching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality peppermint leaves from your garden or purchase them from a reputable supplier in abundance.
Dry the peppermint leaves using a food dehydrator at 90 degrees fahrenheit for eight hours or overnight.
Combine one part dried peppermint leaves with four parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar and seal it tightly.
Allow the mixture to steep for six weeks, shaking the jar daily, before straining and discarding the solids.
Transfer the peppermint tincture to dark glass bottles and store them in the refrigerator for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tinctures helps with jaw clenching because of its calming and relaxing properties. The herb contains compounds like rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid, which work together to soothe tense muscles and reduce anxiety and stress. When we're feeling anxious or overwhelmed, it's common for us to subconsciously clench our jaws, but lemon balm tinctures can help calm the nervous system and alleviate this tension, promoting a sense of relaxation and reducing jaw clenching.
remedy_properties: Relaxation and sedative effects: Herbal lemon balm tincture helps to relax the muscles involved in jaw clenching, including the masseter, temporalis, and medial pterygoid muscles, thereby reducing tension and discomfort.
Antispasmodic properties: Lemon balm tincture's antispasmodic effects help to alleviate muscle spasms and cramps associated with jaw clenching, providing relief from pain and stiffness.

Anti-anxiety benefits: The anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties of lemon balm tincture can contribute to a decrease in stress and anxiety levels, which are common triggers for jaw clenching disorders such as bruxism, thereby reducing the frequency and intensity of jaw clenching episodes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the lemon balm plant are used to make tinctures because they contain a higher concentration of essential oils and compounds that help relax the muscles in the jaw, reducing clenching and tension.

Leaves: Lemon balm leaves are a popular choice for making tinctures due to their mild flavor and aroma, which can be calming and soothing for the jaw and facial muscles, helping to alleviate symptoms of clenching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves ensuring they are free from contaminants and debris.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator or air drying method for 24-48 hours.
Grind dried melissa officinalis leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 part ground lemon balm with 2 parts vodka or high-proof grain alcohol in a clean glass jar for a minimum of 6 weeks.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding solids after preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Skullcap tinctures helps with jaw clenching because it contains a compound called baicalin, which has a calming effect on the nervous system. When taken as a supplement, skullcap tincture can help reduce anxiety and stress, common triggers of jaw clenching. Additionally, skullcap's sedative properties can help relax the muscles in the face and jaw, reducing tension and discomfort associated with bruxism. By promoting relaxation and calming the mind and body, herbal skullcap tinctures offer a natural solution for alleviating jaw clenching symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (anxiety-relieving) properties: Skullcap's anxiolytic properties help to calm the nervous system, reducing tension and anxiety that can contribute to jaw clenching.
Muscle relaxant properties: Skullcap's muscle relaxant properties help to reduce muscle spasms in the face and jaw, relieving tension and discomfort caused by chronic teeth grinding or clenching.

Adaptogenic properties: Skullcap's adaptogenic properties help the body adapt to stress and anxiety, reducing the physical symptoms of stress (such as jaw clenching) and promoting relaxation and calmness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Skullcap roots are rich in bioactive compounds that help relax the muscles and reduce tension in the jaw, making them an effective part for relieving jaw clenching.
Buds: The buds of the skullcap plant contain a high concentration of flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the jaw muscles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh scutellaria lateriflora flowers at peak blooming stage and dry them immediately for optimal potency.
Combine one part dried skullcap flowers with two parts vodka in a clean glass jar for a 50-50 ratio.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 6 weeks, shaking the jar daily to extract optimal compounds.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another glass container, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid.
Bottle the final product in amber glass containers with tight-fitting lids and store it in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for jaw clenching are concentrated liquid extracts made from plants, flowers, and roots that help alleviate tension in the jaw muscles caused by stress, anxiety, or misalignment. These natural remedies work by relaxing the muscles, reducing inflammation, and promoting a healthy nervous system response. Examples of herbal tinctures effective against jaw clenching include valerian root, passionflower, and lemon balm. By using these tinctures, individuals can experience relief from chronic pain and discomfort, allowing them to eat, speak, and smile with ease, ultimately improving their overall quality of life.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat jaw clenching with herbal tinctures is by combining Valerian root and Passionflower. Valerian root tincture helps to relax the muscles and calm the nervous system, while Passionflower tincture soothes anxiety and stress-related tension in the jaw. Mix equal parts of both tinctures in water or take them separately as directed. This combination can effectively alleviate jaw clenching and promote overall relaxation.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to jaw clenching that are treated with herbal tinctures are anxiety, stress-related disorders, and muscle tension. Herbs such as valerian root, passionflower, and chamomile have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate symptoms of overactive muscles like jaw clenching. Other herbs like skullcap and lavender can also be used to soothe digestive issues related to stress, such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).
